GLLG.audit.title: Loot Auditor — Gear Health Check
GLLG.audit.notPF2e: The Loot Auditor requires the Pathfinder 2e system.
GLLG.audit.noSystem: The Loot Auditor has no adapter for the active game system (supported: Pathfinder 2e, D&D 5e 2024).
GLLG.audit.plutoniumRequired: Loot source is set to “Plutonium only”, but the Plutonium module isn't active. Enable Plutonium or change the D&D 5e loot source setting.
GLLG.audit.noParty: No party found. Add characters to a Party/Group actor, or assign characters to players.
GLLG.audit.abpNote: Automatic Bonus Progression is on — fundamentals are expected to come from the character, not runes.
GLLG.audit.estimateNote: Fundamentals are audited against the ABP curve. Wealth is an estimate vs. expected level wealth — coins spent on consumables and services lower the live total, so read drift as directional.
GLLG.settings.shoppingAccess.name: Shopping Access
GLLG.settings.shoppingAccess.hint: How freely the party can buy/craft gear. Sets the baseline core-vs-unusual loot ratio (AoN guidance).
GLLG.settings.variantABP.name: Automatic Bonus Progression
GLLG.settings.variantABP.hint: Enable if this campaign uses the ABP variant (fundamental bonuses come from the character, not runes). Changes how the auditor interprets fundamentals.
GLLG.settings.proficiencyWithoutLevel.hi: Enable if this campaign uses the Proficiency Without Level variant (character level is not added to proficiency-based modifiers and DCs). When on, LLM-authored workshop items are told to scale their modifiers and DCs to the variant's flatter math instead of the by-level baseline.
GLLG.settings.driftTolerance.name: Wealth Drift Tolerance (%)
GLLG.settings.driftTolerance.hint: How far a PC's wealth may stray from the expected curve before the auditor flags them ahead or behind.
GLLG.settings.partyActor.name: Party Actor ID (optional)
GLLG.settings.partyActor.hint: Force the auditor to use a specific Party actor by its id. Leave blank to auto-detect.
GLLG.settings.heirloomMode.name: Heirloom Mode (runes awaken in place)
GLLG.settings.heirloomMode.hint: When on, fundamental-rune rewards climb within each PC's signature weapon instead of dropping as new gear — same RAW price, so budget and the auditor are unaffected. The reward becomes an in-place edit to the held weapon (a story-beat 'awakening').
GLLG.settings.heirloomArmor.name: Heirloom Mode — include armor
GLLG.settings.heirloomArmor.hint: Also awaken armor fundamentals (potency/resilient) within the PC's worn armor. Off = weapons only.
GLLG.settings.etchRunes.name: Etch runes onto weapon & armor loot
GLLG.settings.etchRunes.hint: When on, generated weapon and armor drops come pre-etched with an appropriate, legal, RAW-priced rune set (potency, striking/resilient, and eligible property runes) sized to the party's level and the find's theme. Eligibility follows each rune's Usage restriction (e.g. keen only on piercing/slashing melee, shadow only on light/medium armor), so no illegal combinations are produced. Off = only pre-made magic items drop.
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourceMode.name: D&D 5e loot source
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourceMode.hint: Where the 5e generator draws items from. Auto: prefer Plutonium-imported content, then fall back to any world/SRD pack. Plutonium only: draw exclusively from Plutonium-sourced compendiums (use this to guarantee every find is a Plutonium item). Internal compendiums only: draw exclusively from the dnd5e system's own bundled (SRD) packs — never Plutonium or other modules, and never import on demand.
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourcePack.name: Source Compendia (D&D 5e)
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourcePack.hint: Optional list of specific compendia to draw loot from — one OR MORE, comma-separated, matched against each pack's collection id or label (e.g. 'world.plutonium-items, world.my-homebrew-magic'). When set this RESTRICTS sourcing to just those packs (within whatever the source mode above allows). Leave blank to auto-detect, preferring Plutonium-imported packs and falling back to the dnd5e SRD. Install & import via the Plutonium module for the full 2024 magic-item catalogue.
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourceBooks.name: Allowed sources (D&D 5e)
GLLG.settings.dnd5eSourceBooks.hint: Optional comma-separated allow-list of content sources to draw loot from, matched against each item's source (book code or homebrew name), e.g. 'PHB, DMG, XGE' or 'My Homebrew'. Homebrew names match by substring. Leave blank to use everything Plutonium has imported. This layers on top of the source mode above — it narrows WHICH books/homebrew within your imported content are eligible, so you can run pure-homebrew or core-plus-homebrew loot. Items with no source are excluded while a list is set.
GLLG.settings.dnd5eAutoImport.name: Plutonium auto-import (D&D 5e)
GLLG.settings.dnd5eAutoImport.hint: When on and Plutonium exposes an import API, the generator may ask Plutonium to import catalogue content on demand (e.g. an LLM-named item not yet in a compendium). Off = only index content you have already imported via Plutonium's UI.
GLLG.settings.llmFlavor.name: LLM Flavor & Provenance
GLLG.settings.llmFlavor.hint: When on, each generated hoard makes one call to the claude -p sidecar to add provenance flavor text to its items. Purely cosmetic — prices and rules never change. Any failure drops the loot with plain rules-text (never blocks generation). Requires the sidecar URL below.
GLLG.settings.sidecarUrl.name: Flavor Sidecar URL
GLLG.settings.sidecarUrl.hint: Base URL of the claude -p sidecar. Use a same-origin path reverse-proxied through your Foundry nginx (e.g. /gllg-sidecar) so it stays behind HTTPS and is never internet-facing.
GLLG.settings.sidecarSecret.name: Flavor Sidecar Secret
GLLG.settings.sidecarSecret.hint: Shared secret sent as the x-gllg-secret header. Must match the GLLG_SECRET the sidecar was started with. GM-only.
GLLG.settings.llmModel.name: Claude Model
GLLG.settings.llmModel.hint: Which Claude model the sidecar uses for flavor and workshop generation. Accepts a CLI alias (opus, sonnet, haiku) or a full model id (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6). Leave blank to use the sidecar's own default (its GLLG_MODEL, or the claude CLI default). The model must be available to the Claude login the sidecar runs under.
GLLG.settings.campaignContext.name: Campaign Context (for the LLM)
GLLG.settings.campaignContext.hint: A short blurb describing your campaign — setting, tone, region, recurring villains, signature themes. Fed to the LLM as baseline context so generated flavor, provenance, and custom workshop loot fit your world. Leave blank to keep flavor generic. Only used when the LLM sidecar is enabled.
GLLG.settings.motionTier.name: Animation
GLLG.settings.motionTier.hint: How much motion the GLUniverse loot windows and chat cards use (the 'Etched Glass' look). Reduced: no light sweeps or idle pulses — state changes are plain fades. Default: the tuned, springy motion. Cinematic: slower, richer reveals with trailing flares. Per-screen; your operating-system 'reduce motion' setting always overrides this down to Reduced.
GLLG.llmlog.menuHint: Open a log of recent calls to the LLM sidecar (flavor and workshop) — status, duration, and any error (timeout, HTTP 502, bad response). Handy for diagnosing why a generation failed.
GLLG.llmlog.title: GLUniverse — LLM Call Log
